scanner.skippedCooldownDesc: How long unchanged skipped signals stay suppressed before they can reactivate.

uiLock.enableDesc: Locks the UI after inactivity while backend workers continue running.

vpn.requireVpnDesc: Block all trades if the VPN proxy is unreachable (recommended)
vpn.infoNote: Only actual trading requests (order placement, cancellation) are routed through the proxy. Market scanning, price feeds, and all other data remain on your direct connection for maximum speed.

net.allowDesc: Let other devices on your local network reach the dashboard

positions.assignDesc: Attach this live wallet position to a bot so lifecycle logic can continue managing it.

validation.title: Validation Guardrail
validation.subtitle: Global rules that auto-demote underperforming strategies. Demoted strategies stay loaded but their signals are blocked at the orchestrator's decision gate.
validation.evaluateNowTitle: Re-run the auto-demotion engine right now (uses current thresholds)
validation.autoDemotionEngineDesc: When enabled, the engine evaluates every strategy on its rolling window and flips status to demoted when the directional-accuracy or MAE thresholds break.
validation.autoPromoteOnDesc: Auto-promote is on — strategies that recover get restored automatically.
validation.autoPromoteOffDesc: Auto-promote is off — demoted strategies stay parked until you manually activate them.
validation.minSampleSizeHint: Minimum resolved opportunities required before the engine can demote.
validation.minDirectionalAccuracyHint: Below this and the strategy is demoted. 0.5 = coin flip; 0.52 is a mild edge.
validation.maxMaeRoiHint: Mean absolute error in predicted ROI. Above this and the strategy is demoted.
validation.lookbackDaysHint: Rolling window over which accuracy / MAE are computed.
validation.autoPromoteOnRecoveryDesc: Restore strategies to active when their metrics return to compliance. Manual overrides are never auto-cleared.
validation.clearOverrideTitle: Drop the manual override and let the auto-demotion engine drive it again

mlModels.title: Machine Learning
mlModels.headerNoteDefault: External models load lazily and only affect trading when you activate a deployment.
mlModels.importDesc: Point Homerun at a local artifact and declare the feature contract it should use.
mlModels.name: Name
mlModels.importedReady: Imported model artifact ready for activation.
mlModels.sessionAttribution: Training will use only rows captured during this session. The session id is preserved on the resulting adapter for attribution.

autoresearch.title: Strategy Research
autoresearch.subtitle: Code evolution and L2-realistic backtesting with Cox PH fill model, ensemble PnL bands, and counterfactual queue replay.
autoresearch.tabReverseTitle: Reverse-engineer a wallet's trading strategy via the LLM agent loop
autoresearch.pickStrategyForCode: Pick a strategy to start a code experiment.
autoresearch.studioNeedsStrategy: Backtest Studio needs a strategy selected. Pick one from the dropdown above.
autoresearch.loopDescStart: The LLM proposes a code change → AST validates → backtest runs → if score improves we keep and bump a new strategy version, otherwise revert. Loops up to
autoresearch.loopDescEnd: iterations or until you stop it.
autoresearch.noIterationsYet: No iterations yet. Start an experiment to begin.
autoresearch.configureProviderHint: Configure a provider in Settings → LLM to populate this list.
autoresearch.mandatePlaceholder: e.g. Improve win rate without increasing max drawdown

unifiedStrategies.pnlLiveOnly: Live trades only — submitted to Polymarket
unifiedStrategies.pnlSimOnly: Simulated/shadow trades only — no live rows yet
unifiedStrategies.selectOrCreate: Select a strategy or create a new one
unifiedStrategies.confirmDeleteSystem: Delete system strategy "{{name}}"? It will be tombstoned and will NOT auto-reseed.
unifiedStrategies.runtimeConfigCascade: Edits here update Strategy.config — the defaults every bot inherits when they run this strategy. The runtime cascade is source default_config → Runtime Config (here) → Bot Tune strategy_params; each layer overrides the previous. Per-bot tweaks live in Bots → Tune → Parameters.
unifiedStrategies.healthActiveDesc: Strategy is participating in trading. Signals are being acted on.
unifiedStrategies.healthDemotedDesc: Strategy is parked — signals are still recorded but not acted on. Investigate accuracy / MAE before reactivating.
unifiedStrategies.healthUntrackedDesc: Strategy hasn't accumulated enough data yet to be promoted or demoted.
unifiedStrategies.liveVsSimulatedNote: Live = real venue · Simulated = paper / shadow
unifiedStrategies.calibrationEmpty: No weekly calibration buckets are available for this strategy yet. The trend appears once the validation engine has accumulated at least two completed 7-day windows of resolved opportunities.
unifiedStrategies.forceActivateTitle: Force this strategy to participate in trading regardless of telemetry
unifiedStrategies.parkStrategyTitle: Park this strategy — signals still recorded, but not acted on
unifiedStrategies.dropManualOverride: Drop the manual override and let the auto-status engine drive it again
unifiedStrategies.autoStatusNote: Auto-status uses the metrics above plus configured guardrails. Manual overrides stick until you clear them.
unifiedStrategies.telemetryDesc: Telemetry accumulates as opportunities flow through detect → evaluate → exit. Run a backtest in Research or wait for live signals to populate the metrics.
unifiedStrategies.codeEditorPlaceholder: Write your strategy source code here...
unifiedStrategies.createStrategyDesc: Configure strategy metadata and choose a scaffold. Code editing starts after draft creation.
unifiedStrategies.generateWithAiDesc: Describe the strategy behavior, markets, and risk controls.
unifiedStrategies.aiPromptPlaceholder: Build a momentum strategy on BTC/ETH event markets with volatility filters, configurable edge threshold, and strict stop-loss logic.
unifiedStrategies.modifyStrategyDesc: Generate a revised source draft, review it, then apply it to the editor before saving.
unifiedStrategies.modificationPlaceholder: Example: tighten the exit logic, reduce risk after two losses, and only select positions when liquidity is above the configured threshold.
unifiedStrategies.aiGeneratedDraft: AI generated a modified source draft.
unifiedStrategies.generateToPreview: Generate a draft to preview changes
unifiedStrategies.describeAndGenerate: Describe the modification you want, then generate a revised strategy source draft.
unifiedStrategies.tplFullUnifiedDesc: Detect + evaluate + exit scaffold with complete defaults.
unifiedStrategies.errOverride: Failed to update strategy health override
unifiedStrategies.errClearOverride: Failed to clear strategy health override
unifiedStrategies.errTemplateRequired: Select a template before creating the draft.

strategyReverseEngineer.title: Reverse Engineer
strategyReverseEngineer.noJobsYet: No reverse-engineer jobs yet. Click New job above to start one.
strategyReverseEngineer.pickJobHint: Pick a job on the left or click + New job to start.
strategyReverseEngineer.newJobDesc: The agent will pull the wallet's full trade history, pick a dataset, iteratively write a strategy that mimics it, and backtest until the composite score plateaus.
strategyReverseEngineer.pipelineDesc: The deep iterative agent produces BOTH the PDF report and the Python strategy class as outputs. PDF + code share the same underlying wallet understanding — they're rendered from the iteration history when the agent converges.
strategyReverseEngineer.deepReverseEngineerHint: Iterative LLM agent — hypothesizes → writes BaseStrategy Python → backtests → critiques → refines until target_score or max_iterations. Outputs: PDF report (from iteration history) + Python strategy class (promotable to live).
strategyReverseEngineer.quickAnalyticalHint: Skip the iteration loop — deterministic stat tables (two-leg P/L decomp, dominance buckets, filter ledger) + one LLM call per section. PDF only, no Python class, no backtest. Use when you want a fast brief, not the actual reverse-engineering.
strategyReverseEngineer.dataSourceDesc: What market data the agent backtests against.
strategyReverseEngineer.dataSourceReportDesc: Optional — analytical reports use the wallet trade history directly. Provider/session data is used for deep dives when available.
strategyReverseEngineer.dsAutoHint: Wallet trade window, any data we have
strategyReverseEngineer.noDatasets: No imported datasets — go to Data Lab → Providers first.
strategyReverseEngineer.noSessions: No recording sessions — start one in Data Lab → Record.
strategyReverseEngineer.autoModeNote: Auto mode uses whatever microstructure data you already have for the wallet's trade window. Best-effort with possible coverage gaps. For crypto wallets prefer a Polybacktest provider dataset; for niche event markets capture a recording session first.
strategyReverseEngineer.modelOverrideLabel: Model override (optional — defaults to AI → Models → "Strategy Reverse-Engineer")
strategyReverseEngineer.confirmDelete: Delete reverse-engineer job {{id}}?  This wipes the job and all its iteration rows.  This cannot be undone.
strategyReverseEngineer.deleteTitle: Delete this run and all its iterations
strategyReverseEngineer.noIterationsYet: No iterations yet. The agent submits one per candidate strategy.
strategyReverseEngineer.failedToParse: Failed to parse analytical report payload — try the PDF download instead.

autoresearchView.noDynamicFields: No dynamic parameter fields available for this bot.
autoresearchView.paramExperimenter: Parameter experimenter — operates on this bot's live strategy params.
autoresearchView.codeExperimenter: Code experimenter — evolves strategy source against the backtest data plane.
autoresearchView.noStrategiesConfigured: No strategies configured on this trader
autoresearchView.mandatePlaceholder: e.g. Keep max drawdown below 15%, focus on win rate...
autoresearchView.selectStrategyToStart: Select a strategy and click Start to begin code evolution.
autoresearchView.clickStartToOptimize: Click Start to begin parameter optimization.

backtestStudio.equityCurveEmpty: Equity curve will appear after the first run.
backtestStudio.correlationNoStrategies: No strategies have ≥ 5 terminal trades in the last {{days}} days. Cross-strategy correlation needs daily PnL series to compute.
backtestStudio.correlationLegend: Red cells (ρ > 0.4) ⇒ strategies that drew down together. Green (ρ < -0.4) ⇒ natural hedge pair. Diversification ratio above 70% = healthy portfolio.
backtestStudio.discoveryHybridTip: Discovery: strategy.detect ran against historical market state at sampled time intervals AND we cached opportunities the live system already saw. This is the default backtest mode.
backtestStudio.discoveryHistoricalTip: Discovery: strategy.detect ran against historical market state. No live opportunity history existed for this strategy in the window — the backtest is entirely replay-driven.
backtestStudio.discoveryLiveOppsTip: Discovery: ONLY OpportunityHistory rows the live system already produced. This is fill-counterfactual mode (testing fill-model / risk gates), not full backtest. Set discover_from_history=True to run discovery against recorded data.
backtestStudio.replayDeltaTip: Engine replayed book_delta_events — same data source as the live system (live-parity).
backtestStudio.replaySnapshotsTip: Engine replayed market_microstructure_snapshots. Switch to the delta path by populating book_delta_events (it does so automatically once the live system runs).
backtestStudio.liveParityDetails: engine ran against book_delta_events — same source as live ({{withDeltas}}/{{total}} tokens · median {{deltasMedian}} deltas/hr)
backtestStudio.fidelityMediumDetails: median {{median}} snaps/token/hr · {{withSnaps}}/{{total}} tokens. Taker-mode strategies replay accurately; passive resting GTC limits may underfill.
backtestStudio.fidelityLowHeader: ⚠ DATA FIDELITY: {{rating}} — backtest fills NOT representative of live
backtestStudio.ensembleEmpty: No ensemble samples for this run (no fills had captured book context).
backtestStudio.counterfactualEmpty: No counterfactual replays available for this run (no recorded book deltas in the fill window).
backtestStudio.runHistoryEmpty: No runs yet. Click Run backtest to start.
backtestStudio.studioSubtitle: L2 replay · Cox PH fill model · ensemble PnL bands · counterfactual queue replay
backtestStudio.strategySourceTooltip: Source loaded from the selected strategy. Edit it in Code Experiments.
backtestStudio.windowTooltip: How many days of history to backtest against. Quick = fast iteration (~10-30s); Standard = thicker samples for confidence (~1-2min); Thorough = max statistical power (3-5min).
backtestStudio.presetTitle: {{label}} — {{val}}-day window, expect ~{{eta}} wall-clock at typical scale
backtestStudio.impactTooltip: Square-root impact: bps adverse adjustment when consuming 100% of side depth. 5-10 = deep crypto books; 25-50 = thin event markets. 0 = disabled.
backtestStudio.makerRebateTooltip: Polymarket maker LP-rewards approximation. ~1-3 bps realistic on top crypto markets; >5 bps optimistic. Only paid on inside-band fills.
backtestStudio.paramsHint: Edits apply to this run only · backend merges over the strategy's
backtestStudio.resetTooltip: Restore the strategy's declared default values
backtestStudio.noDynamicParamsHint: Add fields to the strategy's config_schema.param_fields to enable iteration.
backtestStudio.targetScoreTooltip: Stop when best_score (Sharpe × DSR × WF − DD penalty) reaches this. Empty = no target, runs until max_iterations.
backtestStudio.stopNoImproveTooltip: Exit early after this many consecutive non-improving iterations
backtestStudio.autoApplyTooltip: Persist kept overrides to Strategy.config so future runs use them by default
backtestStudio.mandatePlaceholder: e.g. minimize drawdown without sacrificing Sharpe
backtestStudio.stopIterationTooltip: Stop the running iteration; the in-flight backtest still finishes.
backtestStudio.iterateNoParams: Strategy declares no params to iterate
backtestStudio.iteratePickStrategy: Pick a strategy from the dropdown to enable iteration
backtestStudio.iterateOpen: Set target score + start an LLM-driven param search
backtestStudio.backtestRunningBackend: Backtest running on the backend — results will appear here when complete. Safe to switch tabs.
backtestStudio.skeletonRunning: Backtest running on the dedicated worker process. Backend stays responsive; safe to switch tabs.
backtestStudio.emptyPerformanceHint: Pick a strategy and click Run backtest. Headline KPIs, risk-adjusted metrics with deflated Sharpe, and the equity curve appear here.
backtestStudio.emptyFillHint: Run a backtest to populate ensemble PnL bands, counterfactual queue replay, and partial-fill aggregation against the live trade tape.
backtestStudio.emptyRobustnessHint: Run a backtest to populate triangulation, regime decomposition, and walk-forward cross-validation.
backtestStudio.noPortfolioDataHint: The portfolio correlation matrix needs at least 2 strategies with ≥ 5 terminal trades each in the last 30 days. As trades resolve, this view populates automatically.
backtestStudio.outcomeNettingFootnote: Polymarket markets sum to ~$1 per share at resolution; holding all sibling outcomes of a market caps worst-case loss at the redemption guarantee. Rebate shown is conservative (50% of gross of fully-covered groups). Increasing "full coverage" via outcome-aware sizing is how multi-leg strategies scale capital efficiency.
backtestStudio.driftFootnote: Drift = live realized minus backtest expectation. Degraded strategies should be re-backtested or paused; improved strategies suggest the simulator is conservative.
backtestStudio.tailRiskFootnote: ES = mean of worst-tail returns; tail ratio < 1 ⇒ downside-heavy payouts.
backtestStudio.triangulationTitle: Triangulation — backtest vs shadow vs live (last 30 days)
backtestStudio.triangulationFootnote: Δ > 30% between any two regimes ⇒ the fill model is likely the suspect (latency or queue assumptions). Δ < 10% means the simulator is well-calibrated for this strategy.
backtestStudio.partialFillFootnote: Low instant-fill rate ⇒ orders walk the book or queue-decay before completing. High VWAP dispersion ⇒ price moved during the partial fill (slippage cost beyond the headline number).
backtestStudio.regimeFootnote: Lopsided win-rate across one slice (e.g. 80% on Tue but 30% Mon-Fri-Sat) ⇒ strategy works only in one regime. Healthy strategies have flat-ish bars across all four decompositions.
backtestStudio.wfEmpty: Click Run to split the last 14 days into {{folds}} {{mode}} folds and backtest each separately. Stable returns across folds = real edge; high variance = overfit.
backtestStudio.tomFootnote: Same trade set, randomized ordering. Realized Sharpe at p99+ ⇒ a few well-placed trades did all the work; near p50 ⇒ the edge is robust to sequence.
backtestStudio.cpcvFootnote: PBO > 50% ⇒ observed Sharpe likely overfit; PBO < 30% ⇒ edge generalizes across path permutations. Embargo {{seconds}}s.
backtestStudio.cpcvEmpty: Click Run CPCV to evaluate every C({{folds}},{{kTest}}) = {{combinations}} combination of test folds. More rigorous than walk-forward — catches edges that hold up against arbitrary subsets of history, not just one chronological path.
backtestStudio.latencyMcFootnote: Negative slope = edge erodes under worse latency (typical maker behavior). Slope ≈ 0 = strategy is latency-insensitive.
backtestStudio.latencyMcEmpty: Click Run latency MC to backtest at 0.5×, 0.75×, 1×, 1.5×, 2× latency multipliers. Reveals how much your edge depends on the latency assumption — and how a network regression in production would erode it.
backtestStudio.dqNoRejects: ✓ no structural rejects — books pass price-bound, ordering, and sequence checks
backtestStudio.kmBaselineNote: KM baseline — no covariate ratios. Cox will train once new orders accumulate with full survival_features.
backtestStudio.noActiveModel: No active model. Trigger a retrain in Strategies → Machine Learning → Fill Model.
backtestStudio.latencyDetails: {{n}} samples · ensemble uses pessimistic={{pess}}ms / realistic={{real}}ms / optimistic={{opt}}ms
backtestStudio.latencyDefaultsNote: No measured fills yet — values shown are hardcoded fallbacks. Run a strategy long enough to capture submit/cancel timestamps to replace these.
backtestStudio.cancelRateNote: high cancel rate ⇒ spoofy book ⇒ lower effective displayed depth (auto-applied to ensemble)
backtestStudio.providerDatasetEmpty: No imported datasets — go to Data Lab → Providers to add some.

dataLab.title: Data Lab
dataLab.subtitleBrowse: Browse data the orchestrator + scanner have already captured. Filter, preview, export, or hand it to the agent.
dataLab.subtitleRecord: Live market-data ingestor status, proactive WS coverage, and on-demand capture sessions.
dataLab.subtitleStorage: Per-dataset row counts, on-disk sizes, and age windows for everything the system has captured.
dataLab.subtitleProviders: Pull historical market data on demand from third-party providers (polybacktest etc.) into your Data Lab.
dataLab.noRowsMatch: No rows match the current filters.
dataLab.ingestorSub: unified · snapshots + deltas · single hot-path call · persists off-thread
dataLab.ingestorFootnote: Always-on capture from the WebSocket feed. No per-asset config — captures whatever the orchestrator subscribes to. For targeted captures (specific markets, specific windows, specific tick intervals), use the on-demand sessions panel below.
dataLab.proactiveCoverageSub: keeps the WS feed subscribed to liquid catalog markets so backtests have data
dataLab.proactiveFootnote: Pulls top liquid markets from MarketCatalog every {{interval}}s and subscribes the WS feed proactively — every market the ingestor sees writes snapshots (mms) AND deltas (book_delta_events). Without this loop, the ingestor only captures markets the orchestrator/scanner actively touches, leaving strategy opportunity tokens with no historical book data when the backtest runs. Cap ({{cap}}) and floor (${{floor}}) tunable via HOMERUN_RECORDER_MAX_TOKENS / HOMERUN_RECORDER_MIN_LIQUIDITY.
dataLab.cryptoOhlcSub: crypto bars (BTC / ETH / SOL / ...) at fixed timeframes — feeds the crypto ML adapters
dataLab.confirmDeleteAllMlData: Delete ALL recorded ML data? This cannot be undone.
dataLab.noSessionsYet: No sessions yet. Click New session to capture a slice of market data on demand.
dataLab.sessionsFootnote: Each completed session is replayable through the unified backtester — pass session_id on POST /backtest/run and the backtester scopes its replay to the session's target tokens × time window.
dataLab.newSessionSub: Pick markets, capture types, and a window — backtester will replay this slice.
dataLab.name: Name
dataLab.descriptionPlaceholder: capturing book + trade for Trump-Vance debate window
dataLab.targetKindEventHint: expand to all markets in the event
dataLab.captureLegend: book = L2 snapshots · trade = print tape · delta = trade-vs-cancel events
dataLab.errAtLeastOneTarget: At least one target value is required
dataLab.errAtLeastOneCapture: Pick at least one capture type
dataLab.willBeCreatedHint: — click Start on the row to activate
dataLab.confirmDeleteSession: Delete session '{{name}}'?  Captured rows stay in DB.
dataLab.architectureBody: A single in-process LiveMarketDataIngestor is always on whenever the WebSocket feed is alive. It validates each book update once, classifies depth changes as trade vs cancel, and persists both full snapshots (market_microstructure_snapshots) and per-level deltas (book_delta_events) off the hot path. The backtester reads deltas first (live-parity replay) and falls back to snapshots when delta coverage is sparse. No separate recorder process to start; coverage is governed by which markets the WS is subscribed to (see Proactive coverage).

dataLabProviders.title: External data providers
dataLabProviders.subtitle: Pull historical market data on demand from third-party vendors into your Data Lab. Imports land in the same microstructure tables backtests already read from.
dataLabProviders.notImplemented: {{label}} integration not yet implemented in this build.
dataLabProviders.addApiKeyHint: Add your polybacktest API key in Settings → Data Providers to enable import.
dataLabProviders.apiKeyHelp: Get a key at polybacktest.com/dashboard. Empty value clears the key.
dataLabProviders.reverseEngineerDefaultsHelp: Default LLM model is set in AI → Models (under "Strategy Reverse-Engineer").
dataLabProviders.fallbackHelp: Empty fields fall back to ai_default_model + the service-level guards. No defaults baked into code.
dataLabProviders.depthDescription: Always pulls full L2 depth (15 levels per side · UP + DOWN)
dataLabProviders.jobQueued: Job {{id}} queued — watch progress in the Active jobs panel.
dataLabProviders.noImportsYet: No imports yet — pick markets on the left to start.
dataLabProviders.importedDatasetsHint: Available in the Backtest Studio dataset picker. Snapshot rows live in market_microstructure_snapshots.
dataLabProviders.polymarketDescription: Synthesize historical book snapshots from Polymarket's /prices-history endpoint to fill gaps the live WebSocket recorder didn't reach. Inserts into the same MarketMicrostructureSnapshot table the recorder writes to, tagged synthetic so the backtester can downweight.
dataLabProviders.restBackfillBody: Use when WS coverage doesn't reach back far enough. Pick a scope (token / strategy / recording session / top-liquid catalog), a window, and a fidelity — the service synthesizes book snapshots centered on each mid price and inserts them into the same MarketMicrostructureSnapshot table the live recorder writes to. Synthetic rows carry a flag the backtester can read.
dataLabProviders.backfillSub: Synthesize book snapshots from Polymarket /prices-history (mid only).
dataLabProviders.syntheticDataCaveatBody: REST gives mid prices only — best_bid/best_ask are reconstructed by centering on the mid with a configurable spread. Sizes are zero (no depth). Each row is tagged payload_json.synthetic = true so the backtester / Cox PH trainer can filter or downweight.
dataLabProviders.scopeStrategyHint: all tokens this strategy fired on
dataLabProviders.strategySlugHint: Pulls every distinct token from this strategy's OpportunityHistory rows in the time window.

rssSourceForm.urlInvalid: Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL

searchFiltersFlyout.title: Market Settings
searchFiltersFlyout.intro: Global scanner thresholds are configured here. Strategy-specific detection and execution filters are configured below per strategy via StrategySDK config schemas.
searchFiltersFlyout.rejectionDescription: Hard rejection thresholds applied to every opportunity before execution.
searchFiltersFlyout.riskDescription: Global risk model thresholds used when scoring opportunities.
searchFiltersFlyout.strategyManagementDescription: Full strategy source and lifecycle management is in the Strategies tab.
searchFiltersFlyout.strategyManagementBody: Open Strategies to manage all opportunity strategy code and status.

weatherWorkflowSettingsFlyout.title: Weather Workflow Settings
weatherWorkflowSettingsFlyout.enableWorkflowHelp: Turn weather discovery and intent generation on/off
weatherWorkflowSettingsFlyout.autoRunHelp: Run scans on interval without manual trigger
weatherWorkflowSettingsFlyout.strategyCodeHelp: Edit the Weather Edge opportunity strategy source code

poolSettingsFlyout.title: Pool Settings
poolSettingsFlyout.intro: Full runtime configuration for smart-wallet pool selection. Values are persisted and applied by the tracked-traders worker.

accountSettingsFlyout.title: Account Settings
accountSettingsFlyout.privateKeyDescription: Your wallet private key for signing transactions
accountSettingsFlyout.kalshiPasswordHelp: Used for email/password authentication to Kalshi API
accountSettingsFlyout.kalshiApiKeyHelp: Alternative to email/password. If set, API key is preferred for authentication.
accountSettingsFlyout.kalshiInfo: Kalshi credentials enable cross-platform arbitrage trading. The scanner will automatically detect price differences between Polymarket and Kalshi for the same events. You can use either email/password or an API key for authentication.
accountSettingsFlyout.enterChainlinkApiKey: Enter Chainlink Data Streams API key
accountSettingsFlyout.chainlinkUserSecretHelp: Used to HMAC-sign requests against the Data Streams REST API
accountSettingsFlyout.oracleInfo: Optional second oracle source. When configured, prices are polled directly from Chainlink's REST endpoint alongside Polymarket's RTDS-relayed Chainlink feed — the source-comparison panel surfaces the relay-vs-direct delta so you can see when the relay lags. Without credentials the feed stays disabled and never connects.

traderConfigFlyout.descriptionCreate: Configure a new bot profile with explicit strategy, source, risk, and schedule controls.
traderConfigFlyout.descriptionEdit: Update strategy, source, risk, and schedule settings for this bot.
traderConfigFlyout.botProfileSubtitle: Name this bot. The strategy and source are configured below.
traderConfigFlyout.botModeScopeLive: This bot is scoped to live execution only.
traderConfigFlyout.botModeScopeSandbox: This bot is scoped to sandbox execution only.
traderConfigFlyout.name: Name
traderConfigFlyout.latencySlow: Slow — relaxed budgets for multi-leg / recon-heavy strategies
traderConfigFlyout.latencyHelp: Fast-tier bots run in the ``fast_trader_runtime`` with an isolated DB pool and sub-second budgets. They MUST be single-leg strategies (one market per signal). Pick Normal unless you explicitly need sub-second latency.
traderConfigFlyout.noBotsAvailableLive: No live bots available to copy from.
traderConfigFlyout.noBotsAvailableSandbox: No sandbox bots available to copy from.
traderConfigFlyout.copyFromHelp: Creates a new bot with copied source config, strategy params, interval, risk limits, and schedule settings from the selected bot. Trades, decisions, orders, and events are never copied.
traderConfigFlyout.strategySubtitle: Pick the strategy this bot will run. The signal source is derived from the strategy.
traderConfigFlyout.tradingScheduleSubtitle: UTC-only bot gate by days, time window, and optional date bounds.
traderConfigFlyout.scheduleGateOffHelp: When off, this bot can trade any time.
traderConfigFlyout.transferTradesTitle: Transfer open trades to another bot before deleting
traderConfigFlyout.transferTradesHelp: All open positions and active orders from {{name}} will be reassigned to the selected bot, then {{name}} will be permanently deleted.
traderConfigFlyout.forceDeleteLiveBody: This permanently deletes {{name}} while live exposure is still open. Only continue if those positions or orders were already flattened outside Homerun.
traderConfigFlyout.forceDeleteBody: This permanently deletes {{name}} and bypasses the normal exposure safety check.
traderConfigFlyout.understandOrphanLive: I understand this can orphan live exposure if it is still open.
traderConfigFlyout.understandPermanentDelete: I understand this permanently deletes the bot.

cryptoSettingsFlyout.title: Settings
cryptoSettingsFlyout.seriesIdsHelp: Configure 5m/15m/1h/4h BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP series IDs used by the crypto strategy.
cryptoSettingsFlyout.strategyCodeHelp: Edit the BTC/ETH HF opportunity strategy source code

newsWorkflowSettingsFlyout.title: News Workflow Settings
newsWorkflowSettingsFlyout.enableWorkflowHelp: Turn news scanning and intent generation on/off
newsWorkflowSettingsFlyout.autoRunHelp: Run scans on interval without manual trigger
newsWorkflowSettingsFlyout.enableNewsLlmCallsHelp: Disables event extraction, rerank, and edge-estimation LLM calls when off
newsWorkflowSettingsFlyout.strategyCodeHelp: Edit News opportunity strategies in the Strategy Manager
